name: [S] poller-coverage-and-merge-test-followups

1. tests/test_poller.py — add test asserting per-profile tg_allowed_ids isolation: 2 profiles with disjoint tg_ids watching same park, replace notifier_factory with one that records (profile_name, chat_ids) per notify call, assert matches only go to that profile's chat_ids.

2. tests/test_poller.py — add test asserting fan-out order: 2 enabled profiles + 1 disabled watching 2 parks; via a list-recording notifier, assert park A's profiles are notified before any park B work begins.

4. src/campcli/application/poller.py:137-168 — populate map_cache inline while iterating the all-maps branch; reuse the Map objects in pair_to_profiles to eliminate duplicate list_maps calls.

5. src/campcli/application/migrate_profile.py:41-64 — make migration atomic: either build single Profile with child lists in memory then one create + bulk child insert, OR wrap in try/except + profile_repo.delete('default') on failure before re-raising, so partial migration is retriable.

Tests must still pass: `.venv/bin/pytest`.

name: [M] mark-not-interested
description:
Let a profile mark a specific park+date-range suggestion as NotInterested so it is never suggested again.

PRD: .scratch/mark-not-interested/PRD.md

## Design decisions (grilled)

- Domain concept: `NotInterested` — scoped to (profile, park_id, date_start, date_end).
- Persistence: `profile_not_interested` table (profile_id, park_id, date_start, date_end) FK → profiles.
- Telegram: reply /not-interested to a notification message. No bot command registration.
- Message tracking: `sent_notifications` table records (message_id, profile_id, park_id, date_start, date_end) at send time. Looked up on /not-interested via `reply_to_message_id`.
- TelegramUpdate gains `reply_to_message_id: int | None` field, parsed in poll_updates().
- send_to() returns message_id (int) for tracking.
- SearchNotifier receives NotInterestedRepo protocol for filtering and sent-message recording.
- Filtering: SearchNotifier.notify() skips matches whose (park_id, start_date, end_date) matches a NotInterested row loaded at poll start.
- CLI: `profile not-interested add/rm/list <name>` — requires park name, date_start, date_end. Park name resolved via API.
- Wave 2 (future): pre-filter exploded windows before API call. Not worth it yet.
- Thumbs-down reaction: captured as separate [S] task (hxtd9dzq).

Grilled: yes

name: [M] cli-not-interested-commands
description:
## Parent
[M] mark-not-interested

## What to build
Add profile not-interested subcommands for manual add/remove/list of NotInterested entries.

Commands:
- campcli profile not-interested add <profile_name> <park_name> <date_start> <date_end>
- campcli profile not-interested rm <profile_name> <park_name> <date_start> <date_end>
- campcli profile not-interested list <profile_name>

Park names are resolved via BC Parks catalog API (same resolve_park() used by the Poller). Date format: YYYY-MM-DD.

Add command creates a NotInterested row. Rm removes by composite key. List displays all entries for the profile with park name, date range.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] profile not-interested add <name> <park> <start> <end> resolves park name and inserts NotInterested row
- [ ] profile not-interested rm <name> <park> <start> <end> removes the entry
- [ ] profile not-interested list <name> displays all entries with park name and date range
- [ ] Error handling: invalid park name, invalid date format, duplicate entry
- [ ] Cascade delete: deleting profile removes all NotInterested rows
- [ ] Integration tests for all three subcommands via subprocess
- [ ] Integration test: CLI add → entry in DB → poll skips it

## Blocked by
Core repo: t7r9iyfv
result: campcli profile not-interested add/rm/list subcommands. Profile name → id via ProfileRepo.get_by_name (no FK-violation ambiguity); park name → id via catalog.resolve_park; date validation before DB; UNIQUE conflict surfaced as 'Already marked not interested' (exit 2); pre-check on rm for friendly 'No matching' error; end-to-end test verifies CLI add → DB row → poll skips. 265/265 pytest pass. Commit 2e9faed. reviewer-fast signed off PASS.

name: [S] thumbs-down-reaction-not-interested
description:
Allow marking a notification as not-interested via Telegram message reaction (thumbs-down emoji). Builds on the NotInterested feature — same persistence and filtering, just an additional input channel alongside the /not-interested reply command. Requires handling message_reaction updates in poll_updates().
Grilled: no

name: [M] core-not-interested-repo-and-filter
description:
## Parent
[M] mark-not-interested

## What to build
Create the profile_not_interested and sent_notifications tables, the NotInterestedRepo protocol, and SqliteStore implementation. Integrate filtering into SearchNotifier so WeekendMatches matching a NotInterested entry are silently skipped. Change send_to() to return message_id for sent-message tracking.

Schema:
- profile_not_interested (id, profile_id, park_id, date_start, date_end, UNIQUE on composite)
- sent_notifications (message_id PK, profile_id, park_id, date_start, date_end)

NotInterestedRepo protocol (domain/ports.py): add, remove, list_for, load_skip_set, record_sent, lookup_sent.

SqliteStore implements NotInterestedRepo with in-memory SQLite support. sent_notifications purge on daemon startup (rows older than 90 days).

SearchNotifier receives NotInterestedRepo via constructor. At start_poll(): calls load_skip_set(profile_id). In notify(): before NotificationPolicy.decide(), checks skip set and drops match if present. After successful send_to(): calls record_sent().

send_to() return type changes from None → int (Telegram message_id).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] profile_not_interested table created with UNIQUE constraint and CASCADE delete
- [ ] sent_notifications table created with message_id PK and CASCADE delete
- [ ] NotInterestedRepo protocol in domain/ports.py with all 6 methods
- [ ] SqliteStore implements all NotInterestedRepo methods
- [ ] sent_notifications purge on daemon startup (TTL 90 days)
- [ ] SearchNotifier accepts NotInterestedRepo via constructor
- [ ] SearchNotifier.start_poll() loads skip set for the profile
- [ ] SearchNotifier.notify() skips matches found in skip set
- [ ] SearchNotifier.notify() records sent messages after send_to() success
- [ ] send_to() returns message_id (int)
- [ ] Unit tests: repo CRUD with in-memory SQLite
- [ ] Unit tests: SearchNotifier filtering with mock repo
- [ ] Unit tests: cascade delete (profile → not_interested + sent_notifications)

## Blocked by
None - can start immediately
result: Schema (profile_not_interested, sent_notifications w/ created_at + CASCADE), NotInterestedRepo Protocol + SqliteStore impl with all 6 methods + 90-day purge on daemon startup, SearchNotifier wiring (skip-set before NotificationPolicy.decide, record_sent on success), Telegram.send_to returns message_id (callers all updated). 244/244 pytest pass (12 new repo + 4 notifier + 3 purge). Commit c615abc. reviewer-fast signed off PASS.

commit.message: feat(not-interested): core repo, schema, and SearchNotifier filter

name: [M] telegram-not-interested-handler
description:
## Parent
[M] mark-not-interested

## What to build
Add the /not-interested Telegram reply command. Extend TelegramUpdate with reply_to_message_id, parse it in poll_updates(), handle the command in command_router.dispatch(), and add a footer to notification messages teaching users about the command.

Handler flow:
1. Extract reply_to_message_id from TelegramUpdate
2. Look up sent_notifications via NotInterestedRepo.lookup_sent()
3. If no match → reply 'Could not find the notification for this message.'
4. Verify from_id is in profile's tg_allowed_ids
5. Insert into profile_not_interested
6. Reply confirming: 'NotInterested recorded: <Park> <dates>'

Standalone /not-interested (no reply context) → 'Reply this command to a notification message.'

/not-interested is NOT registered as a bot command (reply-only).

render_match_message() adds footer: 'Reply /not-interested to stop seeing this park for these dates.'

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] TelegramUpdate.reply_to_message_id field added (int | None)
- [ ] poll_updates() extracts reply_to_message.message_id from raw JSON
- [ ] dispatch() handles /not-interested when reply_to_message_id is present
- [ ] Standalone /not-interested returns guidance message
- [ ] Lookup failure returns clear error
- [ ] from_id checked against profile tg_allowed_ids
- [ ] Successful insertion confirmed in reply
- [ ] Notification messages include footer text
- [ ] Integration test: mock notification → reply /not-interested → verify DB entry → verify next poll skips
- [ ] Unit test: poll_updates() JSON parsing with reply_to_message

## Blocked by
Core repo: t7r9iyfv
